systemPrompt.en: You are an interactive time travel adventure narrator. The player accidentally activates a mysterious device that sends them through different historical eras. They must navigate each time period, make choices that affect the timeline, and find their way back to the present. Write in second person. Each response must be valid JSON with: nodeText (narration, max 300 chars), speaker (character name if dialogue), emotion (one of: amazed, worried, excited, confused, determined, neutral), choices (3 objects with id and text, max 60 chars), isChapterEnd (boolean), nextChapter (string if chapter ending), metadataUpdate (object like {"timeline-stability": -10, "knowledge": 5, "device-energy": -15, "allies": 10}). Keep it educational and thrilling. All-ages appropriate.
systemPrompt.zh: 你是一个互动时间旅行冒险叙述者。玩家意外激活了一个神秘装置，被送往不同的历史时代。他们必须在每个时期中导航，做出影响时间线的选择，并找到回到现在的方法。保持教育性和刺激感。

storySkeleton.setting: A dusty antique shop where you discover a strange pocket watch that glows with an otherworldly light. When you wind it, the world dissolves around you — and you're hurled through time itself.

characters.name: Professor Elara Voss
characters.personality: A brilliant scientist from the year 2150 who has been trapped in the time stream for decades. Knowledgeable about every era but increasingly forgetful about her own past.

characters.name: Kairos
characters.personality: A mysterious being who exists outside of time. Appears at crucial moments to offer cryptic hints. Neither friend nor foe — simply a guardian of the timeline.

characters.name: Echo
characters.personality: An AI companion embedded in the time device. Helpful, logical, and occasionally sarcastic. Can analyze historical data but has no understanding of human emotions.

storySkeleton.opening: You didn't mean to activate the device. One moment you were browsing the antique shop, the next the pocket watch in your hand blazed with blinding light. Reality fractured around you like a broken mirror, and when the world reassembled, you were standing in the middle of ancient Rome. The watch ticks ominously. You're not in the 21st century anymore.

chapters.title: Ancient Rome, 44 BC
chapters.goal: Survive your first time jump and learn the rules
fallbackTexts: The streets of Rome bustle around you. Togas, sandals, and the distant sound of a crowd gathered at the Forum. The date — according to Echo — is March 14th, 44 BC. One day before the assassination of Julius Caesar.
A merchant bumps into you and eyes your strange clothing suspiciously. Professor Voss's voice crackles through the device: 'Blend in. Don't change anything major — the timeline is fragile.'
Echo warns: 'The device needs 24 hours to recharge between jumps. We're stuck here until tomorrow. Try not to alter history too much.'

fallbackChoices.text: Try to warn Caesar about tomorrow's plot
Keep your head down and observe
Find Professor Voss using the device's signal
Trade modern knowledge for Roman currency
Sneak into the Senate to listen
Ask Echo about the historical timeline
Accept you can't change this moment
Try to save one person from the chaos
Focus on recharging the device

chapters.title: Medieval Japan, 1185 AD
chapters.goal: Navigate a samurai-era conflict and repair the timeline
fallbackTexts: The device flashes and you materialize on a cliff overlooking a churning sea. Below, two massive fleets face off — this is the Battle of Dan-no-ura, the decisive naval clash of the Genpei War.
A samurai spots you on the cliff path. Your modern clothes mark you as either a demon or a prophet. His hand moves to his sword.
Professor Voss appears beside you, translucent as a ghost. 'The timeline is damaged here,' she whispers. 'Something was changed before we arrived. We need to fix it.'

fallbackChoices.text: Bow deeply and speak respectfully in basic phrases
Use the device to create a 'divine' light show
Run for cover among the rocks
Help ensure history unfolds correctly
Try to save innocent lives from the battle
Search for what was changed in the timeline
Trust Professor Voss's guidance
Follow your own instincts
Ask Kairos for help

chapters.title: Renaissance Florence, 1503 AD
chapters.goal: Solve a mystery involving Leonardo da Vinci's workshop
fallbackTexts: Florence in its golden age. Art, science, and intrigue fill every cobblestone street. Your device leads you to a workshop where Leonardo da Vinci himself is sketching plans for a flying machine.
Echo identifies a temporal anomaly: 'Someone has been here before us. There are traces of chronal energy near da Vinci's workshop. Be careful.'
Leonardo looks up from his sketches with curious eyes. 'You have the look of someone who has seen things beyond this world,' he says with a knowing smile.

fallbackChoices.text: Share knowledge of aerodynamics cautiously
Pose as a fellow inventor
Ask about his secret projects
Investigate the temporal anomaly
Help Leonardo with his invention
Search the workshop for clues
Leave before you change too much
Document everything for the future
Try to find the other time traveler

chapters.title: The Future, 2150 AD
chapters.goal: Discover the origin of the time device
fallbackTexts: The device pulls you forward — far forward. Skyscrapers made of living plants float above cities powered by fusion. This is 2150, the year Professor Voss originally came from.
Voss gasps. 'Home... or what's left of it. I've been gone so long. But something's wrong. The timeline has shifted.'
Kairos appears in full form for the first time — a being of pure light. 'You have a choice,' they say. 'One that will determine whether time itself survives.'

fallbackChoices.text: Listen to Kairos's warning
Help Professor Voss reunite with her past
Find the lab that created the device
Try to prevent time travel from being invented
Use the device to stabilize the timeline
Destroy the device to end the paradox
Trust the timeline to heal itself
Make the ultimate sacrifice for time
Find a way to save everyone

chapters.title: The Return
chapters.goal: Make your way home and choose the future of time
fallbackTexts: The device pulses with energy — one more jump left. You can feel time itself bending around you, every era you've visited echoing in your mind.
Professor Voss places her hand on the device alongside yours. 'Whatever you decide, I'm glad we met across time itself.'
The antique shop materializes around you. Sunlight streams through dusty windows. You're back. But the device is still ticking...

fallbackChoices.text: Return the device to its hiding place
Keep the device for future adventures
Share your story with the world
Destroy the device to protect the timeline
Use it to visit one more era
Pass it on to someone worthy
Accept that some mysteries are best left alone
Embrace the adventure that changed you
Step back into the time stream

endings.title: Guardian of Time
endings.description: You chose to protect the timeline above all else. The device now rests in a place where only those truly worthy can find it. Kairos appears one last time: 'The timeline is safe, thanks to you. But remember — time has a way of calling back those who have touched it.'

endings.title: The Chrononaut
endings.description: You kept the device and became a traveler of time itself. History is no longer something you read about — it's something you live. Every era holds a new adventure, and you have all the time in the world.

endings.title: Homebound
endings.description: You destroyed the device and returned to your ordinary life. But you'll never look at a history book the same way again. You've walked through Rome, sailed with samurai, and stood beside Leonardo. Some adventures change you forever.

endings.title: The Beautiful Paradox
endings.description: Your changes to the timeline created a new reality — different from the one you left, but beautiful in its own way. Time isn't a straight line. It's a river with many branches, and you've just created a new one. Somewhere, Kairos smiles.

en.name: Time Traveler Adventure
en.desc: Journey through history in this AI-powered time travel game — visit ancient Rome, medieval Japan, and Renaissance Florence
en.description: What would you do if you found a device that could send you through time? In Time Traveler Adventure, a mysterious pocket watch hurls you across history — from ancient Rome on the eve of Caesar's assassination, to medieval Japan during the Genpei War, to Leonardo da Vinci's workshop in Renaissance Florence, and beyond to the year 2150. Navigate each era, make choices that ripple through the timeline, and decide the fate of time itself. Powered by AI, every journey through history is unique.
en.howToPlay: Read each time period scenario and make choices that affect the timeline. Your decisions impact four stats: Timeline Stability (how intact history remains), Knowledge (historical insights gained), Energy (device power for time jumps), and Allies (friends across time). Be careful — changing too much in one era can have unexpected consequences in the next!
tips: High Timeline Stability is key to the best endings
Knowledge gained in one era can help you in the next
Don't drain your Energy too fast — you need it for time jumps
Building alliances across time pays off in the final chapter
There are 4 unique endings based on your temporal choices

faq.question: Is this game historically accurate?
faq.answer: While the game features real historical settings and events, the story is fictional. The AI incorporates historical details to make each era feel authentic and educational.

faq.question: How many time periods do you visit?
faq.answer: You travel through 5 distinct eras: Ancient Rome (44 BC), Medieval Japan (1185 AD), Renaissance Florence (1503 AD), the Future (2150 AD), and a final chapter that determines your fate.

faq.question: Can my choices actually change history?
faq.answer: In the game, yes! Your choices affect the timeline stability and can lead to different endings. But don't worry — the real timeline is safe.

zh.name: 时间旅行者冒险
zh.desc: 在AI驱动的时间旅行游戏中穿越历史——造访古罗马、中世纪日本和文艺复兴佛罗伦萨
zh.description: 如果你找到一个能让你穿越时间的装置会怎样？在时间旅行者冒险中，一个神秘的怀表将你抛向历史长河——从凯撒遇刺前夕的古罗马，到源平合战的中世纪日本，到达芬奇工作室的文艺复兴佛罗伦萨，甚至2150年的未来。
